hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The generated output must contain version 310.

context_pointers.vague_reference: malformed text that cannot be parsed as SPIR-V
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Any sequence of characters starting with arbitrary text (e.g., 'this is not...') that lacks valid SPIR-V binary header (0x07230203) and subsequent instructions.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_compile_to_glsl.json.description: Validate a SPIR-V module and cross-compile it into a GLSL program. The input is a SPIR-V assembly listing for the requested textual target; on success the output is a header line block followed by the verbatim generated GLSL source.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          %1 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
               OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
               OpEntryPoint Vertex %main "main" %outColor %vtxColor
               OpSource ESSL 310
               OpName %main "main"
               OpName %outColor "outColor"
               OpName %vtxColor "vtxColor"
               OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
               OpDecorate %vtxColor Location 0
       %void = OpTypeVoid
          %3 = OpTypeFunction %void
      %float = OpTypeFloat 32
    %v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
   %outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_ptr_Input_v4float = OpTypePointer Input %v4float
   %vtxColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v4float Input
       %main = OpFunction %void None %3
          %5 = OpLabel
         %12 = OpLoad %v4float %vtxColor
               OpStore %outColor %12
               OpReturn
               OpFunctionEnd

feature2_compile_to_hlsl.json.description: Validate a SPIR-V module and cross-compile it into an HLSL program. On success the output is the header block followed by the verbatim generated HLSL source.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          %1 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
               OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
               OpEntryPoint Vertex %main "main" %outColor %vtxColor
               OpSource ESSL 310
               OpName %main "main"
               OpName %outColor "outColor"
               OpName %vtxColor "vtxColor"
               OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
               OpDecorate %vtxColor Location 0
       %void = OpTypeVoid
          %3 = OpTypeFunction %void
      %float = OpTypeFloat 32
    %v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
   %outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_ptr_Input_v4float = OpTypePointer Input %v4float
   %vtxColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v4float Input
       %main = OpFunction %void None %3
          %5 = OpLabel
         %12 = OpLoad %v4float %vtxColor
               OpStore %outColor %12
               OpReturn
               OpFunctionEnd

feature3_compile_to_msl.json.description: Validate a SPIR-V module and cross-compile it into a Metal Shading Language program. On success the output is the header block followed by the verbatim generated MSL source.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          %1 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
               OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
               OpEntryPoint Vertex %main "main" %outColor %vtxColor
               OpSource ESSL 310
               OpName %main "main"
               OpName %outColor "outColor"
               OpName %vtxColor "vtxColor"
               OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
               OpDecorate %vtxColor Location 0
       %void = OpTypeVoid
          %3 = OpTypeFunction %void
      %float = OpTypeFloat 32
    %v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
   %outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_ptr_Input_v4float = OpTypePointer Input %v4float
   %vtxColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v4float Input
       %main = OpFunction %void None %3
          %5 = OpLabel
         %12 = OpLoad %v4float %vtxColor
               OpStore %outColor %12
               OpReturn
               OpFunctionEnd

feature4_compile_to_vulkan.json.description: Validate a SPIR-V module and re-emit it as a Vulkan SPIR-V binary module. Unlike the textual targets, the result is a binary word stream; the output reports the binary container magic number and the number of 32-bit words produced.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          %1 = OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"
               OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
               OpEntryPoint Vertex %main "main" %outColor %vtxColor
               OpSource ESSL 310
               OpName %main "main"
               OpName %outColor "outColor"
               OpName %vtxColor "vtxColor"
               OpDecorate %outColor Location 0
               OpDecorate %vtxColor Location 0
       %void = OpTypeVoid
          %3 = OpTypeFunction %void
      %float = OpTypeFloat 32
    %v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%_ptr_Output_v4float = OpTypePointer Output %v4float
   %outColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Output_v4float Output
%_ptr_Input_v4float = OpTypePointer Input %v4float
   %vtxColor = OpVariable %_ptr_Input_v4float Input
       %main = OpFunction %void None %3
          %5 = OpLabel
         %12 = OpLoad %v4float %vtxColor
               OpStore %outColor %12
               OpReturn
               OpFunctionEnd

feature5_reject_invalid_module.json.description: Reject a source that is not a valid SPIR-V module. Whether the input is empty or syntactically meaningless, and regardless of the requested target, the request is rejected with a normalized error category instead of producing any program output.

input.assembly: this is not valid spirv assembly
this is not valid spirv assembly

feature6_1_webgpu_to_glsl.json.description: Cross-compile a module authored for the WebGPU execution environment into GLSL. The module is first transformed from WebGPU to the Vulkan environment, then validated and cross-compiled; on success the output is the header block followed by the generated GLSL source.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          OpCapability VulkanMemoryModelKHR
          OpExtension "SPV_KHR_vulkan_memory_model"
          OpMemoryModel Logical VulkanKHR
          OpEntryPoint Vertex %func "shader"
%void   = OpTypeVoid
%void_f = OpTypeFunction %void
%func   = OpFunction %void None %void_f
%label  = OpLabel
          OpReturn
          OpFunctionEnd

feature6_2_webgpu_to_hlsl.json.description: Cross-compile a WebGPU-environment module into HLSL via a WebGPU-to-Vulkan transform followed by validation and cross-compilation.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          OpCapability VulkanMemoryModelKHR
          OpExtension "SPV_KHR_vulkan_memory_model"
          OpMemoryModel Logical VulkanKHR
          OpEntryPoint Vertex %func "shader"
%void   = OpTypeVoid
%void_f = OpTypeFunction %void
%func   = OpFunction %void None %void_f
%label  = OpLabel
          OpReturn
          OpFunctionEnd

feature6_3_webgpu_to_msl.json.description: Cross-compile a WebGPU-environment module into Metal Shading Language via a WebGPU-to-Vulkan transform followed by validation and cross-compilation.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          OpCapability VulkanMemoryModelKHR
          OpExtension "SPV_KHR_vulkan_memory_model"
          OpMemoryModel Logical VulkanKHR
          OpEntryPoint Vertex %func "shader"
%void   = OpTypeVoid
%void_f = OpTypeFunction %void
%func   = OpFunction %void None %void_f
%label  = OpLabel
          OpReturn
          OpFunctionEnd

feature6_4_webgpu_to_vulkan.json.description: Transform a WebGPU-environment module to the Vulkan environment and re-emit it as a Vulkan SPIR-V binary; the output reports the binary magic number and word count.

input.assembly:
OpCapability Shader
          OpCapability VulkanMemoryModelKHR
          OpExtension "SPV_KHR_vulkan_memory_model"
          OpMemoryModel Logical VulkanKHR
          OpEntryPoint Vertex %func "shader"
%void   = OpTypeVoid
%void_f = OpTypeFunction %void
%func   = OpFunction %void None %void_f
%label  = OpLabel
          OpReturn
          OpFunctionEnd

_constraints.input_condition: The input assembly string is provided via JSON under 'assembly'
_constraints.output_rule: The engine must attempt to assemble text lines into a binary SPIR-V module. If successful, the module is validated immediately. If validation fails (syntax, structure, or semantic mismatch), the program halts before any translation.

_constraints.input_condition: The assembled module is NULL, empty, or fails binary validation
_constraints.output_rule: The system must output the specific error block defined below, regardless of the requested target or source environment.

_constraints.input_condition: A rejection occurs (invalid or empty assembly)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be printed to stdout in exact line order. No other text, separators, or code generation occurs.

_constraints.input_condition: Module is valid and target is a text-based language (glsl, hlsl, msl)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must follow the literal 3-line header followed by a separator and the source code.

_constraints.input_condition: Module is valid and target is 'vulkan'
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be a header block followed by binary metadata lines. No source code content.

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains 'target': 'glsl' and a valid SPIR-V assembly
_constraints.output_rule: The engine generates GLSL code. It must detect source version (e.g., 'ESSL 310' becomes '#version 310 es'). Output follows Feature 4 structure.

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains 'target': 'hlsl' and a valid SPIR-V assembly
_constraints.output_rule: The engine generates HLSL code. Input 'geometric' memory model is default float4. Output follows Feature 4 structure.

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains 'target': 'msl' and a valid SPIR-V assembly
_constraints.output_rule: The engine generates Metal Shading Language code. Output includes specific header includes and namespace preamble followed by shader struct/entry point.

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains 'target': 'vulkan' and a valid SPIR-V assembly
_constraints.output_rule: The engine re-clarifies the SPIR-V binary (using 'vulkan' flavor), emits magic number 0x07230203, and calculates the total 32-bit word count.

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains an optional field 'source_env' with value 'webgpu'
_constraints.output_rule: Trigger a pre-compilation transformation. The module must be transformed from WebGPU-capability set to 'VulkanMemoryModelKHR' + 'Logical VulkanKHR' memory model before passing to the validation and target translation logic.

_constraints.input_condition: 'assembly' field is present in JSON but has a value that is null or an empty string (length 0)
_constraints.output_rule: This counts as an invalid module. The system must execute the Error Handling (C002) and Output Structure: Error Block (C003).

_constraints.input_condition: 'assembly' field contains arbitrary text that cannot be parsed as a SPIR-V assembly
_constraints.output_rule: The assembler fails to produce a binary module. The system must execute the Error Handling (C002) and Output Structure: Error Block (C003).

_constraints.input_condition: All success and error cases
_constraints.output_rule: Lines must be printed in the exact order specified by the feature description. No trailing newlines on error lines; non-empty newlines after source output (implied by multi-line examples suggesting standard code block formatting or strict 0-trailing; however, the error block examples do not show trailing newlines after the final target line).

_constraints.input_condition: 'source_env': 'webgpu' is present AND output target is 'vulkan'
_constraints.output_rule: The magic number will correspond to the transformed binary size. The word count must reflect the transformed module (e.g., 39 words for the provided test case).

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains a target
_constraints.output_rule: Output must not include the literal word 'a': any correct output must strictly use the correct target string (glsl, hlsl, Vulkan) from the JSON.

_constraints.input_condition: Target is 'vulkan'
_constraints.output_rule: The engine must emit header lines. No stdout error is acceptable for success. The engine must output 'magic' and 'word_count'.

_constraints.input_condition: Source assembly contains 'OpSource ESSL 310'
_constraints.output_rule: The generated GLSL output must start with the line above GLSL version 310. The engine must detect the embedded version.

_constraints.input_condition: Target is 'msl' and module is valid
_constraints.output_rule: The generated output must contain exactly these lines in order: include <metal_stdlib>, include <simd/simd.h>, using namespace metal;

_constraints.input_condition: Target is 'hlsl' and module is valid
_constraints.output_rule: The generated output must contain 'vert_main()' in the inner function and bubble it through 'main()'.

_constraints.input_condition: Assembled module fails validation
_constraints.output_rule: The result must be exactly the 'error' block. No partial header ('status' or 'target') is allowed to be printed before failure detection.

_constraints.input_condition: Same valid module, different target (glsl vs hlsl vs msl)
_constraints.output_rule: Each target must produce its own specific output format. The format must not be interchangeable. E.g., GLSL 'vec4' is not HLSL 'float4'.

_constraints.input_condition: Input has 'source_env': 'webgpu' and target is 'glsl' or 'hlsl' or 'msl'
_constraints.output_rule: The transformation handles the source before validation. It is not required to preserve the 'webgpu' environment in the output, but to translate the module.

_constraints.input_condition: 'source_env' is present but NOT 'webgpu' (e.g., glsl or 'default') or absent
_constraints.output_rule: The pre-validation transformation is skipped. The module is passed directly to the target translator.

_constraints.input_condition: 'assembly' field contains text like 'this is not valid spirv assembly'
_constraints.output_rule: This path is treated as invalid. Output must be 'error=invalid_module' block.

_constraints.input_condition: 'assembly' field is an empty string
_constraints.output_rule: This path is treated as invalid. Output must be 'error=invalid_module' block.

_constraints.input_condition: Target is 'msl' and module is valid
_constraints.output_rule: The entry point function name must include the source entry point name or be derived as per the test case. For the test case, it is 'shader()'.

_constraints.input_condition: Module is invalid or empty
_constraints.output_rule: The 'stage' field must be 'cross_compile'. The 'target' field must echo the requested target exactly.

_constraints.input_condition: Target is 'vulkan' and module is valid
_constraints.output_rule: The 'magic' field must output '0x07230203'.

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON contains 'target'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'target' value must be one of glsl, hlsl, msl, or vulkan.

_constraints.input_condition: Based on test case 'feature6_1_webgpu_to_glsl'
_constraints.output_rule: The output must match 'status=success', 'target=glsl', '---', '#version 450', 'void main()', '}'.

hide.replacement: expect a small positive integer word count matching transformed size
check for embedded GLSL version string within assembly
hide.oracle_response: The generated output must contain version 310.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: malformed text that cannot be parsed as SPIR-V
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Any sequence of characters starting with arbitrary text (e.g., 'this is not...') that lacks valid SPIR-V binary header (0x07230203) and subsequent instructions.
